executive_summary: Build workflow dispatch failed with HTTP 404 error. The GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build.yml) is not accessible or does not exist in the repository. This is a critical configuration error preventing any build execution.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The workflow dispatch API endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates the workflow file does not exist at the expected path or the GitHub token lacks necessary permissions.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The build.yml workflow file is missing from the .github/workflows/ directory in the scib_demo repository on the main branch, or the GitHub token does not have workflow/actions scope permissions to access or dispatch workflows.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be triggered or executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation including compilation, testing, and deployment.

steps: 2. Create build.yml workflow file with Maven build configuration
3. Configure workflow_dispatch trigger to enable manual/API triggering
4. Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' scope enabled
5. Test workflow dispatch API call after file creation
recommended_fix.workflow_template:
name: Build

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      source_folder:
        description: 'Source folder to build'
        required: false
        default: '.'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean install
        working-directory: ${{ github.event.inputs.source_folder || '.' }}
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Workflow file creation requires repository write access. Audit log will record file creation timestamp, author, and initial configuration. Token permissions must be verified against organization security policies.

errors.title: GitHub Token Permission Insufficient
errors.description: The GitHub personal access token may lack the required 'workflow' scope to dispatch GitHub Actions workflows via API.
errors.error_message: Token missing workflow/actions permissions - API returned 404 instead of 403, suggesting endpoint not found rather than forbidden, but permission issues can manifest as 404.
errors.root_cause: Personal access token may not have 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow_dispatch events via the GitHub API.
errors.impact: Unable to programmatically trigger builds via API. Manual workflow triggers from GitHub UI may still work if workflow file exists, but automated CI/CD pipeline integration is blocked.

steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Locate the configured personal access token
4. If missing, regenerate token with workflow scope
6. Test API call with updated token
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Token scope changes must be logged in security audit trail. New token generation requires approval from repository administrator. Token rotation policy: 90 days maximum lifetime.

compliance_audit.analysis_method: Automated error classification from build failure logs
compliance_audit.manual_review_reason: Critical configuration error requires verification of repository structure and token permissions before automated remediation.
compliance_audit.business_impact: Complete CI/CD pipeline outage. No builds can be executed until workflow file is created and token permissions are verified.

step_2.expected_outcome: Workflow file created and committed to main branch
step_2.rollback_plan: Delete workflow file if validation fails

step_3.command: Test API call to workflow dispatch endpoint

next_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with Maven build configuration
2. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' scope enabled
3. HIGH PRIORITY: Test workflow dispatch API call after fixes
4. MEDIUM PRIORITY: Implement workflow file validation in pre-commit hooks
5. LOW PRIORITY: Add monitoring/alerting for workflow dispatch failures
